description: - (1) Conduct hazard analysis
- Identify known hazards
- Identify reasonably foreseeable hazards
- Evaluate hazards using data, experience, reports
- Determine hazards requiring preventive controls
- (2) Hazard analysis must be written
- Biological hazards
- Chemical hazards
- Physical hazards
- Occur naturally
- Unintentionally introduced
- Intentionally introduced for economic gain
- (1) Evaluate identified hazards; - (i) Assess severity and probability of hazard occurrence
- (1) Evaluate identified hazards; - (ii) Evaluate environmental pathogens for ready-to-eat foods
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (i) Formulation of the food
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (ii) Facility and equipment condition, function, design
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (iii) Raw materials and other ingredients
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (iv) Transportation practices
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (v) Manufacturing/processing procedures
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (vi) Packaging and labeling activities
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (vii) Storage and distribution
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (viii) Intended or reasonably foreseeable use
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (ix) Sanitation, including employee hygiene
- (2) Consider effects on safety of finished food; - (x) Any other relevant factors (e.g., temporal/natural toxins)
